About the Conference

A focused gathering for Europe’s class-action community

Business of Class Actions Europe brings together the people behind collective litigation across Europe to discuss how collective actions work in practice — from building and funding cases to certification, settlement and distribution.

Designed as a practical, business-oriented forum, the conference focuses on the questions that matter: what makes collective actions succeed, where they fail and how different stakeholders are shaping the market across Europe.

Hosted at World of Wine in Porto, the event combines a focused programme with meaningful networking and one of Europe’s most active collective redress jurisdictions.

1
Day of sessions & networking
80+
Curated participants
20+

7
Panel discussions
What Delegates Leave With
Practical insights into how collective actions are built, funded and managed.
A better understanding of certification, economic evidence and claims distribution.
First-hand perspectives from funders, judges, economists and qualified entities.
Benchmarking across Europe’s collective redress landscape.
New connections with senior professionals from across the industry.
Ideas and strategies you can apply in practice.
Who You’ll Meet
General Counsel and litigation leaders
Partners leading collective actions
Litigation funders and damages experts
Qualified entities and regulators
Economists and claims specialists
In-house teams evaluating exposure
Conference Venue
World of Wine, Porto

A 19th-century port-wine cellar district overlooking the Douro. Seven museums, a dozen restaurants, a wine school. The kind of venue that makes people want to stay longer.

Rua do Choupelo 39, 4400-088 Vila Nova de Gaia, Portugal
25 min by car from Porto Airport
Metro D line to Jardim do Morro, 5 min walk
02  Business of Class Actions Europe 2027
Programme

Day One

Pre-Conference Evening
Welcome Reception
Join fellow delegates for an informal evening of Portuguese wine, local flavours and first conversations before the conference begins.
Venue to be confirmed
Thursday, 18 February 2027
18:30 – 21:00
Friday, 19 February 2027
08:30

Morning Coffee & Registration
09:00

Opening Remarks
09:15

PanelThe State of Collective Redress in Europe: A Data-Driven Map

This session sets the factual baseline for the conference. A comparison of selected jurisdictions: claims filed, cases dismissed, average time to certification and more.
10:05

PanelThe Negative Space: Why Collective Redress Fails to Launch

This discussion explores where collective redress was poorly implemented, dead on arrival, or never adopted, and why: procedural, economic, institutional barriers or simply a lack of incentives.
10:55

Networking Break
11:25

PanelQualified Entities as Claimants: Is the Association the Only Door In?

Collective redress in Europe often depends on associations, but what happens when they move too slowly, lack resources or choose not to act? Practical realities, incentives and limitations of the qualified entity model.
12:15

PanelThe Economic Expert’s Role

Economic evidence increasingly influences collective actions from the earliest stages. Data requirements, methodology and how expert analysis can affect the viability of a claim.
13:05

Lunch
14:05

PanelTechnology and Class Actions

Collective litigation increasingly means managing data at scale. Document collection, data processing, practical challenges for legal teams and how courts expect complex evidence to be presented.
14:55

PanelDistribution: Paying Out the Money

Judgment or settlement is not the end of the story. Distribution mechanisms, costs, unclaimed funds and the practical realities of getting compensation into claimants’ hands.
15:45

Networking Break
16:15

PanelThe Litigation Funders’ Perspective

Not every claim is investable. How funders assess opportunities, where they see growth and how economics, risk and regulation influence funding decisions in practice.
17:05

Closing Remarks & Networking

End: 17:15
19:00

EveningThe Porto Evening

Portuguese dinner, live Fado and Port wine in a relaxed atmosphere overlooking Porto. Included in the conference ticket.

Registration

Attendance is intentionally limited to maintain a high-quality mix of perspectives. Passes are allocated on a first-come, first-served basis. All prices exclude VAT.

05  Business of Class Actions Europe 2027
Partner with BOCA27

Be part of the conversation

Business of Class Actions Europe brings together the people behind collective litigation across Europe. Our partnership opportunities are designed to help organisations connect with senior decision-makers, build new relationships and strengthen their position in the European class actions market.

Impressions from Business of Competition 2026, Prague

Bronze Partner
€3,500
Brand presence with the right audience.
  • Logo on website, programme & venue signage
  • 2 delegate passes
  • Quarter-page profile in the conference programme
  • Recognition in pre-event communications
  • Delegate list (subject to consent)
Silver Partner
€5,000
In the room and in the programme.
Everything in Bronze, plus
  • Opportunity to nominate a speaker or panel participant
  • Recognition during opening remarks
  • 3 delegate passes
  • Half-page profile in the conference programme
  • Branded materials in delegate packs
  • Enhanced visibility throughout the conference
Gold Partner
€8,000
Thought leadership and ownership of a moment.
Everything in Silver, plus
  • Keynote speaking slot
  • Exclusive sponsorship of The Porto Evening or Welcome Reception (subject to availability)
  • 5 delegate passes
  • Full-page profile in the conference programme
Individual moments — Welcome Reception, Coffee Break, Workshop, Networking Dinner — also available as standalone packages. All partnerships are tailored. Prices exclude VAT.
Looking for something different?
Every partnership is tailored to your objectives. We’d be happy to discuss how your organisation can contribute to the programme and connect with the right audience.
